results.title: Top Google Trends Alternatives (2026)
results.content:
Trends, not “trending”: Glimpse won't help you much in detecting fleeting, "trending" news or celebrity events. Rather, it's built to identify and pull insights on emerging, sustainable trends.

No site or technical analysis: Doesn't allow you to input your website and get customized recommendations on how to improve performance.

### Price

Free plan provides up to 10 free searches per month, with additional access starting at $99/mo.

### Who should use Glimpse?

Glimpse is the best Google Trends alternative for someone who wants to identify emerging search trends and pull rich keyword insights.

Get started with Glimpse for free.

## Google Trends vs Semrush

Semrush is a comprehensive SEO toolkit that provides keyword research functionality via its “Keyword Overview” tool. [...] Moz’s Keyword Explorer: No trajectory over time

Answer the Public: Built for long-tail ideation, doesn’t provide searches over time/growth metrics.

Mention: Provides social media data, not search.

BuzzSumo: Provides social media data, not search.

Trendfeed: Content/news trends, not search.

Keywords Everywhere: Provides site and social media data, not search-driven keyword research.

Treendly: Provides curated trends rather than being an interactive tool.

Trendhunter: Provides curated reports rather than being an interactive tool.

### FAQ

## What is the best Google Trends alternative?

Glimpse is the best alternative to Google Trends due to its addition of absolute search volume, long-tail data, superior trend discovery, advanced tracking, and more. [...] We've thoroughly tested and compared each potential Google Trends alternative to find out. Below, we explore the top options ranked by their ability to complement or replace Google Trends.

## Comparison chart

google-trends-alternatives-comparison-chart.png

Glimpse

SEMrush

ahrefs

Ubersuggest

Pinterest Trends

Keyword Tool

## Glimpse

Glimpse Google Trends Supercharged

Glimpse (Google Trends Supercharged) is a Chrome extension that enriches Google Trends with absolute search volume on the y axis, long-tail data, superior trend discovery, advanced tracking, and more. It has over 150,000 users, including companies like Amazon, Coca-Cola, IKEA, Chanel, and The New York Times, and has a 4.9 star rating.

### Google Trends vs Glimpse

# 7 Top Glimpse Trends Alternatives (2026)

author photo

#### Stop Guessing, Start Growing 🚀

Use real-time topic data to create content that resonates and brings results.

Contents:

Share:

Google

Glimpse is trend-spotting tool that enhances the features of Google Trends.

undefined

undefined

It fills in a lot of the gaps that exist in Google Trends, such as providing search volume estimates and letting users find trends from an existing database. [...] That said, it's not the only trend detection tool available for marketers, e-commerce stores, investors, and agencies.

Whether you want a different extension experience, are seeking a community, or want to focus specifically on e-commerce trends, these 7 Glimpse alternatives are worth a look.

### Build a winning strategy

Get a complete view of your competitors to anticipate trends and lead your market

Exploding Topics Logo

### 1. Exploding Topics

Exploding Topics is trend detection tool with a huge database of topics, products, and brands.

trends-database-ai.webp

trends-database-ai.webp

Rather than operating on top of the standard Google Trends website, Exploding Topics is a standalone platform that looks at trend popularity across: [...] ### 4. JungleScout

JungleScout is an Amazon sales optimization platform that includes a product trend spotting tool and a Chrome extension.

undefined

undefined

If you're interested in Google Trends Supercharged for Amazon e-commerce purposes, then JungleScout may be a better option.

The tool allows you to see:

If you sell on Amazon, this data can help you:

JungleScout only provides data about Amazon product trends, though. If you're selling on another e-commerce platform, you'll be better served by another tool on this list.

#### How Much Does JungleScout Cost?

There isn't a free JungleScout plan. To use the service, you'll need to opt in to a subscription starting at $29 per month.

#### Glimpse vs. JungleScout: At a Glance

results.title: How to find trends with Glimpse (Google Trends Extension) A full review | Andrew Marketing
results.content:
Topic Map

The topic map gives a quick overview of related topics. It’s a really nice visual to quickly see adjacent topics. The bubble layout does a good job of helping you quickly understand the popularity of related topics.

Channel Information

The channel information is a small feature which might prove useful to those wanting to get a steer on what channels to focus on. It does a good job of showing which platform a topic is most talked about on.

## How does it compare to other tools?

As a marketer it’s tricky to position Glimpse against other tools as it sits alone in terms of features.

The closest alternative is Exploding Topics, however there are differences between the two.
